The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kukurihime|Kukurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kukurihime|Kukurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kurihara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kurihara shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Kamitakara Town, Miyahara 350 | coordinates={{Coord|36.286|N|137.372083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[Itakeru no Mikoto]], and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Main Festival=May 3 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kukurihime|Kukurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.

The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kurihara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kurihara shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Kamitakara Town, Miyahara 350 | coordinates={{Coord|36.286|N|137.372083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[Itakeru no Mikoto]], and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Main Festival=May 3 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} }}
'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|WD=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kukurihime|Kukurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kukurihime|Kukurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|qid=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kikurihime|Kikurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kurihara Shrine’s Cedar Tree''' - Designated as a Takayama City Cultural Property.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/index.htm |title=Natural Monuments |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>
== Access == * Take the {{ill|Nōhi Bus|ja|濃飛バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōhi Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 濃飛乗合自動車|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566124}} Kamitakara/Hirayu Line and get off at "Miyahara" bus stop.

== Enshrined Deities == * [[Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|Itakeru no Mikoto]] (五十猛神) * [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Ōkami]] * [[Ukanomitama no Kami|Uka no Mitama no Kami]] * {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]] * [[Kikurihime|Kikurihime no Kami]] * [[Kagu-tsuchi|Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kurihara Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Kurihara shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Kamitakara Town, Miyahara 350 | coordinates={{Coord|36.286|N|137.372083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[Itakeru no Mikoto]], and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | consecration_year=Unknown | Main Festival=May 3 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|qid=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} }}
'''Kurihara Shrine''' (栗原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, within the Kamitakara area (formerly {{ill|Yoshiki District|en|Yoshiki District|qid=Q8056119|lt=Yoshiki District|1=Yoshiki District}}, [[Kamitakara Village]]). It is situated near the Kamitakara Branch Office of Takayama City (formerly the Kamitakara Village Office).
The shrine is identified as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|qid=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}, being one of eight such shrines in the region.
During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was known as {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}. In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1870), it was officially recognized as the Shikinaisha Kurihara Shrine and renamed accordingly.
